seoTitle: How to Customize Workflows in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to edit a Jira workflow, add or remove statuses and transitions, and match the process to how your team actually works.
intro: Jira's default workflow doesn't always match how a specific team actually moves work forward. This guide covers customizing a workflow so its statuses and transitions reflect your real process.

steps.heading: Open the workflow editor
steps.body: Navigate to your project's workflow settings, typically found in the project or issue type configuration area.

steps.heading: Review the current workflow
steps.body: Look at the existing statuses and transitions to understand what's already in place before making changes.

steps.heading: Add or remove statuses
steps.body: Adjust the statuses to match the actual stages your team's work passes through, adding new ones or removing ones that don't apply.

steps.heading: Configure transitions between statuses
steps.body: Set up the allowed transitions between statuses, controlling how an issue can move from one stage to the next.

steps.heading: Publish the workflow
steps.body: Save and publish your changes so the updated workflow takes effect on live issues.

faq.question: Will customizing a workflow affect issues already in progress?
faq.answer: It can, since existing issues need to map to a valid status in the new workflow, so Jira will typically prompt you to handle any status mapping during publishing.

faq.question: Can different issue types have different workflows?
faq.answer: Yes, Jira lets you assign different workflows to different issue types within the same project if their processes differ.

faq.question: Do I need admin permissions to edit a workflow?
faq.answer: Yes, workflow editing is typically restricted to project or Jira administrators.

faq.question: Can I test a workflow change before publishing it live?
faq.answer: Jira's workflow editor lets you make and preview changes in draft form before you publish them to the live project.
